failures.test: phase3-v194-animation.spec.ts — Phase 3 — Reduced Motion Compliance: page loads without error under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce
failures.error: locator('main, [role=main], body > div').first() resolved to <div hidden=''>
failures.hypothesis: The app uses Next.js SPA routing — there is no <main> element. The fallback selector also resolves to a hidden Next.js portal div. The page renders correctly (other tests pass), but this test has a flawed selector.
failures.suggested_fix: Replace locator('main').first() with a content-specific selector like page.locator('[data-testid="app-shell"], .sidebar, nav').first() or use page.locator('body').toBeVisible().

failures.test: phase3-v194-animation.spec.ts — Phase 3 — Mobile Drawer (Vaul): mobile viewport: page renders correctly without overflow
failures.error: locator('main, [role=main], body > div').first() resolved to <div hidden=''>
failures.hypothesis: Same root cause as reduced-motion test above. The overflow check itself passed (bodyWidth=390px, no overflow), but the subsequent 'main content visible' assertion uses the same flawed <main> selector.

failures.test: phase1-v194.spec.ts — Phase 1 — Sidebar Navigation: sidebar has Events link
failures.hypothesis: REGRESSION — Phase 2 evidence shows this test passed (20/21 in phase1Regression). Screenshot confirms sidebar still shows 'AI Assistant' and 'Dashboards' instead of 'Events' and 'Logs'. Phase 3 appears to have reverted or broken the sidebar nav item renaming from Phase 1.
failures.suggested_fix: Verify sidebar nav items in Phase 3 deployment. The sidebar should have Events (replacing AI Assistant) and Logs links per the Phase 1 spec. Screenshot evidence: sidebar shows 'AI Assistant' and 'Dashboards' nav items.

failures.test: phase1-v194.spec.ts — Phase 1 — Sidebar Navigation: sidebar has Logs link
failures.hypothesis: REGRESSION — Same as Events link above. Sidebar shows 'Dashboards' instead of 'Logs'. This is the same Phase 1 regression.
failures.suggested_fix: Same fix as Events link — restore sidebar nav items 'Events' and 'Logs' that were implemented in Phase 1.

failures.test: responsive.spec.ts — Responsive — Mobile Viewport: mobile nav first tap should navigate immediately
failures.error: getByRole('link', { name: /^settings$/i }) resolved to 2 elements (strict mode violation)
failures.hypothesis: FLAKY — Failed in Round3 AND Phase1. Two Settings links exist in DOM (sidebar nav + mobile header). Strict mode violation. The sidebar now likely shows both a data-testid='nav-item-settings' and a mobile title='Settings' link.
failures.suggested_fix: Use page.getByTestId('nav-item-settings') instead of getByRole('link', { name: /^settings$/i }) to avoid the 2-element strict mode violation.

failures.test: responsive.spec.ts — Responsive — Mobile Viewport: mobile nav tap lands on target route without login returnUrl drift
failures.hypothesis: REGRESSION linked to sidebar Events/Logs regression. This test navigates to /events via sidebar, but the sidebar no longer has an 'Events' nav item (shows 'AI Assistant' instead). Same root cause as phase1-v194 failures.
failures.suggested_fix: Fix sidebar nav items to restore Events/Logs links (same fix as phase1-v194 failures above).

failures.test: optimistic-ui.spec.ts — Optimistic UI + Motion Animations: page navigation renders with transition
failures.hypothesis: FLAKY — Failed in Round3 AND Phase1. The test navigates to /alerts and expects h1 containing 'Alerts'. The Alerts page may render the title differently (e.g. not as h1, or with different capitalization, or the nav click goes to a different route). Could be timing issue.
failures.suggested_fix: Use waitForURL(/\/alerts/) after navigation instead of h1:has-text. The h1 may not exist on the Alerts page — use [data-testid='page-title'] or page.locator('h1, h2').first() with a broader selector.

criticalRegressions: sidebar Events link missing (Phase 1 regression introduced by Phase 3)
sidebar Logs link missing (Phase 1 regression introduced by Phase 3)
responsive Events nav fails due to missing Events sidebar link

notes.phase3AnimationTests: 11/13 passed. 2 failures are test-bugs (wrong selector for Next.js SPA layout — no <main> element exists)
notes.sidebarRegression: CRITICAL: Phase 3 appears to have reverted sidebar nav items. Screenshot shows 'AI Assistant' and 'Dashboards' in sidebar instead of 'Events' and 'Logs' (Phase 1 nav items). This passed in phase2 evidence.
notes.flakies: optimistic-ui.spec.ts:9 and responsive.spec.ts:45 are confirmed flaky (3 consecutive version failures)
notes.byokSkipped: byok-flow.spec.ts — 1 test skipped (env-blocked: DB schema drift, known since phase2)
notes.podActionsSkipped: pod-actions.spec.ts — 4 tests skipped (no live K8s pods, expected for test environment)
